verses.text: All Israel gathers to David at Hebron and anoints him king, 'according to the word of the LORD by Samuel.' The long-delayed promise is fulfilled. David did not seize the throne but waited until God gave it through the people's consent.

All Israel gathers to David at Hebron and anoints him king, 'according to the word of the LORD by Samuel.' The long-delayed promise is fulfilled. David did not seize the throne but waited until God gave it through the people's consent.

All Israel gathers to David at Hebron and anoints him king, 'according to the word of the LORD by Samuel.' The long-delayed promise is fulfilled. David did not seize the throne but waited until God gave it through the people's consent.

David takes the stronghold of Jebus, which becomes Jerusalem, the city of David, with Joab leading the assault. The fortress others could not hold falls to him. God prospers His chosen king to establish the city where His name would dwell.

David takes the stronghold of Jebus, which becomes Jerusalem, the city of David, with Joab leading the assault. The fortress others could not hold falls to him. God prospers His chosen king to establish the city where His name would dwell.

David takes the stronghold of Jebus, which becomes Jerusalem, the city of David, with Joab leading the assault. The fortress others could not hold falls to him. God prospers His chosen king to establish the city where His name would dwell.

David dwells in the stronghold and 'waxed greater and greater: for the LORD of hosts was with him.' The secret of his rising is named. His greatness was not his own doing but the presence of God going with him.

David dwells in the stronghold and 'waxed greater and greater: for the LORD of hosts was with him.' The secret of his rising is named. His greatness was not his own doing but the presence of God going with him.

David dwells in the stronghold and 'waxed greater and greater: for the LORD of hosts was with him.' The secret of his rising is named. His greatness was not his own doing but the presence of God going with him.

The chief mighty men who rallied to David are honoured, like Eleazar who held a field of barley alone when others fled, and 'the LORD saved them by a great deliverance.' Faithful courage stands its ground; the victory is God's.

Three break through enemy lines to bring David water from Bethlehem's well, but he pours it out to the LORD, unwilling to drink what was won at the risk of their lives. He treats their devotion as too sacred to consume. The best love is offered up to God.

Abishai, chief of the three, won a name by his spear against three hundred. Great valour earns honour even short of the highest rank. There is a place of esteem for all who serve God's cause bravely.
Abishai, chief of the three, won a name by his spear against three hundred. Great valour earns honour even short of the highest rank. There is a place of esteem for all who serve God's cause bravely.

Benaiah slew lion-like men of Moab, a lion in a pit on a snowy day, and a giant Egyptian, so David set him over his guard. Courage that acts in the worst conditions is rewarded with trust. God fits the faithful for greater service.

The roll of David's mighty men begins, naming each warrior and his town. Every name is set down. God remembers those who give their strength to His king; the humblest soldier has his place in the record.

More of the mighty men are listed, gathered from across Israel. The catalogue honours faithful service one by one. In God's book no loyal servant is anonymous, however little known on earth.

The list continues with warriors from many regions, even foreigners among them. David's cause drew men from far and wide. So the kingdom of David's Son gathers servants from every people and place.

More names are added to the roll of the valiant. The record presses on, sparing none its honour. Faithfulness to God's anointed, in any age, is written down and not forgotten.

The roll closes with the last of the mighty men, including foreigners who served the king. Their devotion is remembered to the end. God honours all who throw in their lot with His chosen one, whatever their origin.
